# Parity Ledger

How pleco-xa relates to librosa, stated precisely. Everything in the
**Verified** table is machine-checked in CI against fixtures generated by
pinned librosa (see `tools/parity/`). Everything in the **Exceptions** table
is a deliberate, documented divergence — not a gap we're hiding.

## Verified (fixture-gated in CI)

| Area | Functions | Fixture set | Since |
|---|---|---|---|
| Windows | hann, hamming, blackman (periodic / fftbins=True) | `windows.json` | Wave 1 |
| Spectral core | stft magnitude, istft round-trip, fft/ifft | `stft.json`, `istft_roundtrip.json` | Wave 1 |
| Frequencies | fft_frequencies | `fft_frequencies.json` | Wave 1 |
| Conversions | hz_to_mel / mel_to_hz (slaney + htk), hz_to_midi / midi_to_hz, amplitude/power↔db, frames/time/samples conversions | `conversions.json` | Wave 1 |
| Weighting | A/B/C/D frequency weighting (exact IEC constants) | `weighting.json` | Wave 1 |

## Planned (fixture sets exist or land with their wave)

- Mel filterbank + melspectrogram + MFCC (`mel_filterbank.json` committed; specs land Wave 4)
- onset_strength, tempo, beat_track (Wave 2)
- chroma, spectral descriptors (Wave 4)
- effects (trim/split/time_stretch), HPSS (Wave 5)
- DTW, recurrence, segmentation (Wave 5)

## Exceptions (deliberate divergences)

| librosa | pleco-xa position |
|---|---|
| `librosa.display.*` | Replaced by canvas-native `display/` — matplotlib semantics do not apply in a browser. |
| `audioread` multi-codec loading | Browser: `decodeAudioData` covers it. Node: built-in WAV decode + injectable decoder hook. |
| `decompose.decompose` (NMF) | Out of scope pending a correct implementation; the merged marathon NMF converges incorrectly and is not exported. |
| `numba`/`joblib` caching & acceleration | Not applicable; a WASM backend may arrive post-2.0 behind the same API. |

## House rules

- A function is "supported" only when its fixture spec passes in CI.
- Quality gates never silently fall back to fast paths, and no API fabricates
  results (no default BPMs, no fake confidences). Failure paths throw.
